title: Figure 1. Steps to perform antiSMASH analysis for bacteria
author: Rekadwad, Bhagwan
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: Figure 1.    Steps to perform antiSMASH analysis for bacteria: [A] An overview of the bacterial version of antiSMASH shows a data input window where you can either upload the genome sequence in FASTA format or get the genome sequence if it has already been deposited in NCBI (https://antismash.secondarymetabolites.org/ assessed on Feb. 7, 2023, at 16:44:21 h).   [B]   Analysis output: The user has the option of receiving an email notification for the analysed genome. Alternatively, users can either bookmark job links or copy and save a job link to view it further. In both conditions, data can be viewed for a month as per convenience.   [C] The genome   of    Actinopolyspora mortivallis  DSM 44261 (NZ_KB913024.1) indicates that different colour codes correspond to B) regions of the identified secondary metabolite (Regions 1–25). [D] Graphical representation of ectoine gene clusters (region 1 and region 15) taking part in various cellular processes.
